questions.text: When a packet exceeds the MTU, what happens?
options: Fragmentation occurs at the Internet Layer

questions.text: Where are fragmented packets reassembled?
What is the purpose of TCP's three-way handshake?
options: Establish a reliable connection and synchronize sequence numbers

questions.text: What does TCP use to detect packet loss without waiting for timeout?
options: Fast retransmit triggered by duplicate ACKs

options: Determines when to switch from slow start to congestion avoidance

questions.text: What does the TCP 'RST' flag do?
What is TCP's maximum segment size (MSS) derived from?
options: MTU minus IP and TCP headers

questions.text: How does TCP handle out-of-order segments?
options: Buffers them until missing segments arrive

questions.text: What is the TCP keepalive timer used for?
options: Check if idle connection is still alive

questions.text: What is the typical TCP retransmission timeout algorithm?
What does the 'CWR' and 'ECE' flags in TCP header indicate?
What problem does TCP timestamps option solve?
What is TCP SACK (Selective Acknowledgment)?
options: Allows receiver to acknowledge non-contiguous blocks of data

questions.text: What triggers TCP to send an ACK?
options: Delayed ACK timer or every second segment

questions.text: What is the purpose of the TCP 'PUSH' flag?
options: Instructs receiver to deliver data immediately to application

questions.text: How does TCP prevent the Sorcerer's Bug (old duplicate segments)?
What is the difference between flow control and congestion control?
options: Flow control prevents receiver overload; congestion control prevents network overload
Congestion control is only for IPv6

questions.text: What field in TCP header is used for flow control?
What happens when TCP receives an ACK for data not yet sent?
What is TCP's initial congestion window (cwnd) typically set to?
What does TCP NewReno improve over Tahoe?
options: Better recovery during multiple packet losses

questions.text: How does TCP prevent silly window syndrome?
options: Receiver advertises nonzero window only when significant space available

questions.text: Which port does HTTP use by default?

options: Implicit uses dedicated port 990; explicit uses STARTTLS on port 21

questions.text: What does HTTP 404 status code mean?

Map domain name to IP address

questions.text: What type of DNS record maps a domain to an IPv6 address?

options: POP3 downloads and deletes from server; IMAP keeps messages on server

questions.text: Which protocol uses port 22 by default?

options: Active mode server initiates data connection; passive mode client initiates

questions.text: What does HTTP/2 primarily improve over HTTP/1.1?

options: DNS server fully resolves the query contacting other servers

questions.text: What port does SNMP use?
What is the length of a MAC address in bits?
What is the purpose of CSMA/CD in Ethernet?
What replaced CSMA/CD in modern switched Ethernet?
options: Full-duplex operation with switches eliminates collisions

questions.text: What does a switch do when it receives a frame with unknown destination MAC?
options: Floods the frame out all ports except the receiving port

questions.text: What is the purpose of ARP?
options: Resolve IP address to MAC address
Resolve MAC address to IP address

questions.text: What is gratuitous ARP used for?
options: Announce IP-to-MAC mapping to update switch tables

questions.text: What is the Ethernet frame's maximum payload size (MTU)?
What is the purpose of VLANs?
What does the Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) prevent?
What is the OUI part of a MAC address?
What type of cable uses twisted pairs to reduce electromagnetic interference?
What is the maximum distance of Cat6 Ethernet cable at 1 Gbps?
What is the difference between a router and a layer 3 switch?
options: Layer 3 switch routes in hardware (ASIC) for speed; router offers more features

questions.text: What is jumbo frame?
options: Ethernet frame > 1500 bytes MTU, typically 9000 bytes

questions.text: What is PoE (Power over Ethernet)?
options: Delivers electrical power through Ethernet cables

questions.text: You ping a remote server and receive 'Destination Host Unreachable'. What is the most likely cause?
options: No route to the destination network
Server is powered off but route exists

questions.text: A user can browse HTTP websites but HTTPS sites time out. What is the likely issue?
options: Port 443 blocked or TLS handshake failing

questions.text: Two hosts on the same switch cannot communicate. What command would you use first on each host?
You need 100 subnets with 50 hosts each from 172.16.0.0/16. What subnet mask satisfies both?
Traceroute to 8.8.8.8 shows hops but no response from the final destination. What is likely happening?
A VoIP call has choppy audio but web browsing is fine. What is the probable cause?
options: Jitter and packet loss affecting real-time UDP traffic

questions.text: A company has 2000 devices and uses 10.0.0.0/8 with no subnetting. What problem will arise?
options: Huge broadcast domain causing performance issues

questions.text: You get IP 169.254.x.x. What does this indicate?
A file transfer over TCP is very slow but ping RTT is low. What should you check?
options: TCP window size and packet loss causing window reduction

questions.text: Two routers cannot establish OSPF adjacency. What is a common cause?
A website loads partially with broken images and missing CSS. What is the likely issue?
options: Some resources blocked or path errors

questions.text: What does a sustained high rate of TCP retransmissions indicate?
A user reports no internet but can ping 8.8.8.8 by IP. What is the likely problem?
What does a TCP zero window repeatedly followed by small window announcements suggest?
options: Receiver application not consuming data fast enough

questions.text: A subnet with mask 255.255.255.192 has network address 192.168.1.64. What is the broadcast address?
Which statement about TCP is FALSE?
options: TCP guarantees packet delivery within a specific time

questions.text: True or False: UDP always delivers packets faster than TCP.
options: False - UDP generally has lower latency but no guarantee

questions.text: Which of the following does NOT happen during TCP three-way handshake?
True or False: Private IP addresses are routable on the public internet.
options: False - Private addresses are non-routable on internet
False - Only if NAT is used

questions.text: What is a common misconception about ping?
options: Ping succeeding does NOT guarantee application connectivity (ports may be blocked)

questions.text: Which statement about MAC addresses is FALSE?
options: MAC addresses change when moving to different networks
MAC addresses are burned into NIC

questions.text: True or False: A /31 subnet (255.255.255.254) has 0 usable host addresses.
options: False - /31 can have 2 hosts (point-to-point links per RFC 3021)

questions.text: Which is NOT a responsibility of the TCP/IP Transport Layer?
True or False: A router changes the source MAC address of packets it forwards.
options: True - MAC address changes at each hop

questions.text: What is a common misunderstanding about the OSI model vs TCP/IP?
options: TCP/IP has fewer layers but combines some functions
OSI model is obsolete and never used
OSI model is for wireless only

questions.text: Which statement about port 0 is correct?
options: Port 0 is reserved and cannot be used

questions.text: True or False: A switch learns MAC addresses from the destination address of frames.
options: False - Switches learn from source MAC addresses

questions.text: What is wrong with IP address 255.255.255.255?
options: It's the limited broadcast address, not assignable to hosts

questions.text: True or False: TCP guarantees that data will arrive in exactly one segment as sent.
options: False - TCP may segment and reassemble; application sees byte stream

questions.text: Which is NOT a type of DNS server?
True or False: The Internet and World Wide Web are the same thing.
options: False - Internet is network infrastructure; Web is service on it

questions.text: What is a misconception about IPv6?
options: IPv6 is not just more addresses but also better security and autoconfiguration
IPv6 is only about larger address space

questions.text: Which statement about NAT is FALSE?
options: NAT can break some protocols like IPsec

questions.text: True or False: A host with IP 127.0.0.1 can communicate with other hosts on the network.
options: False - Loopback only talks to itself
